title: podman build --cap-add/--cap-drop has no effect and value is not validated, contrary to buildah behavior
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Passing `--cap-add` or `--cap-drop` to `podman build` has no effect and its value is not validated. This is contrary to buildah, where it is validated and has an effect (see "additional information" below), so this does *not* appear to be a buildah bug.

For simplicity, only `--cap-add` is referred to here, but all applies also to `--cap-drop`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a Dockerfile like:
   ```
   FROM fedora:31
   RUN capsh --print && exit 1
   ```

2. Run `podman build --cap-add=cap_sys_admin .` (tested also: `--cap-add=sys_admin` and all caps variants of both).

**Describe the results you received:**

Note omission of cap_sys_admin.

> Current: = cap_chown,cap_dac_override,cap_fowner,cap_fsetid,cap_kill,cap_setgid,cap_setuid,cap_setpcap,cap_net_bind_service,cap_net_raw,cap_sys_chroot,cap_mknod,cap_audit_write,cap_setfcap+eip
>
> Bounding set =cap_chown,cap_dac_override,cap_fowner,cap_fsetid,cap_kill,cap_setgid,cap_setuid,cap_setpcap,cap_net_bind_service,cap_net_raw,cap_sys_chroot,cap_mknod,cap_audit_write,cap_setfcap
>
> Ambient set =cap_chown,cap_dac_override,cap_fowner,cap_fsetid,cap_kill,cap_setgid,cap_setuid,cap_setpcap,cap_net_bind_service,cap_net_raw,cap_sys_chroot,cap_mknod,cap_audit_write,cap_setfcap

**Describe the results you expected:**

Note presence of cap_sys_admin.

> Current: = cap_chown,cap_dac_override,cap_fowner,cap_fsetid,cap_kill,cap_setgid,cap_setuid,cap_setpcap,cap_net_bind_service,cap_net_raw,cap_sys_chroot,cap_sys_admin,cap_mknod,cap_audit_write,cap_setfcap+eip
>
> Bounding set =cap_chown,cap_dac_override,cap_fowner,cap_fsetid,cap_kill,cap_setgid,cap_setuid,cap_setpcap,cap_net_bind_service,cap_net_raw,cap_sys_chroot,cap_sys_admin,cap_mknod,cap_audit_write,cap_setfcap
>
> Ambient set =cap_chown,cap_dac_override,cap_fowner,cap_fsetid,cap_kill,cap_setgid,cap_setuid,cap_setpcap,cap_net_bind_service,cap_net_raw,cap_sys_chroot,cap_sys_admin,cap_mknod,cap_audit_write,cap_setfcap

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

- `podman build` seems to do no validation of the value of `--cap-add`, e.g. `--cap-add=nonsense` continues without error.
- `podman run` performs validation of its `--cap-add` and, unlike buildah, permits omitting the `cap_` prefix.
- `buildah` does not have this problem, so this does not appear, on the surface, to be a buildah bug. E.g. running `buildah run --cap-add=cap_sys_admin $(buildah from fedora:31) capsh --print` includes cap_sys_admin. Further, passing `--cap-add=cap_nonsense` to buildah gives an error.
- Since the buildah version bundled with my podman is a bit older, I also tested against buildah 1.14.0-dev (the only other version currently in my Fedora repos) and `--cap-add` is functioning in that version.

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.14.6
Go Version:      go1.14
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.1-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.3.1
Git Commit:      
Built:           Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version`:** 

```
Version:            1.8.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  SwapFree: 8438935552

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical.

comments.body: @amarshall I ran a quick test against the latest from upstream and it's working for me.  Can you easily clone from GitHub and then run it?  I think this may have been recently fixed.

I installed Podman from the COPR referenced from [install docs](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation#fedora) which appears to be from latest git (1593d4c0) and can confirm `--cap-add` and `--cap-drop` work as expected.

-----

However, unrelated to this issue, running that latest podman as root even for a simple run, e.g. `sudo podman run -it --rm fedora` fails with:

```
ERRO[0000] oci runtime "runc" does not support CGroups V2: use system migrate to mitigate 
Error: this version of runc doesn't work on cgroups v2: OCI runtime error
```

Not sure why that is or if it warrants another issue (or if there is one). Running under my own account works.

Switch to crun for an OCI runtime.  runc does not yet support cgroup V2.
What OS are you running on?

@rhatdan I suspect it’s a packaging problem with the Podman from the aforementioned COPR repository, as it removes several dependencies (conmon, libvarlink-util, podman-plugins) compared with the Fedora packaged podman. I’m on Fedora 32.
Then please use the packages from the distro.

That’s what I do normally, but for the purposes of testing if this bug had been resolved in an unreleased version I used the development COPR repository listed in the [Podman installation docs](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation#installing-development-versions-of-podman).

Anyway, testing on Podman 1.9.1 I can confirm this is resolved.
